name: Bell Peppers Stuffed with Green Chile, Corn, and Hominy
recipeIngredient: 2 Teaspoons Sweet Pepper Oil (left over from prepping peppers)
2 Cups corn kernels, either thawed frozen kernels, or cut from 3 to 4 cobs
1/2 Cup white posole (hominy) corn, canned, well drained
1 Tablespoon diced, canned green chile or charred, peeled, seeded Poblano chile
2 Tablespoons vegetable stock, if you have it on hand, or water, if you don't
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
3 Large green bell peppers, prepared for stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Over medium heat, in a Pam sprayed or nonstick skillet, heat the oil.
2. Add onion and saute about 2 minutes, or until onion is starting to give off an aroma.
3. Add cumin seed, and saute another 2 to 4 minutes, or until onion is beginning to wilt and seeds are fragrant.
4. Add corn, and stir another 3 minutes.
5. Add posole, green chile, garlic, and 1 tablespoon of vegetable stock (or water).
6. Dissolve cornstarch in the remaining tablespoon of stock, and stir into the skillet.
7. The filling will quickly thicken just enough to stick together somewhat, as if glazed.
8. Taste for seasoning, and pile into pepper halves.
Yield: 6 stuffed pepper halves
